text: Pipe utility functions.
Pipe direction types
@table
any
incoming
outgoing
@table
Returns a table of shapes on the path between the requesting shape and target container shape.
params: The direction to fetch in. Defaults to [sm.pipeGraph.direction, sm.pipeGraph.direction.any] (Optional)
return: shapes			The shapes on the path between requesting shape to target. Requester not included in table.

text: Returns a chest which allows for collection of specified items of the specified quantities.
params: The shape connected to the pipe graph who requests the information.
The uuids of items to check.
The number of items of each uuid. Needs to match the number of uuids.
return: container		The shape of the container which was the first one that allows the collection.

text: Returns a chest which allows for spending of specific item with a given quantity.
params: The shape connected to the pipe graph who requests the information.
The uuid of the target item.
return: container		The shape of the container which was the first one that allows spending.

text: Returns a table of all connected input containers sorted by closest first. If the asking shape doesn't have input and output directions it returns all connected containers.
params: The shape connected to the pipe graph who's connected chests you want.
return: containers		A table of connected container shapes sorted by closest first.

text: Returns the remaining of special condition, nil if no data is available.
return integer:	remainingTicks	The remaining ticks of the special condition.
Returns the containers of all chests connected to the given shape through the pipe connections. Will only return containers from shapes of the same type as the original.
params: The interactable where the search will start from.
return: containers				The container of the originInteractable as well as those of the same type connected through pipes.

text: Returns a table of all connected output containers sorted by closest first. If the asking shape doesn't have input and output directions it returns all connected containers.
params: The shape connected to the pipe graph who's connected chests you want.
return: containers		A table of connected container shapes sorted by closest first.

text: Releases any glow and UV overrides made by the shape.
Removes all stored automated tasks capable of producing the specified item from a producing shape.
Removes any stored automated tasks for the requesting shape.
Requests a glow and UV override for pipes between requesting shape and target container.
params: The uv frame index for the affected pipes. Ranges 0 to 3.
The glow multiplier of the pipes.

text: Sets an automated task to be performed by the pipe graph when the body is unloaded.
params: The shape which requests the automated process.

A table with information on the recipe. Uses the crafter recipe format. Can have "craftTime", "quantity", "itemId", "externalContainers"(containers targeted outside pipe graph for consumption, example: refinery), "selfOutputContainer"(if the shape has it's own collection container), "ingredientList", "randomCraftList" random weighted crafts (see prospector), "specialCrafting" used to target specific container slots with custom information (see ore crusher).
Whether the craft tasks should be carried out in parallel or not, off by default. A parallel task will be carried out independently of other automated tasks on this shape. (Optional)

text: Sets special conditions for allowing an interactable to craft.
params: Values for special conditions to allow crafting. Uses "uuid", "tickFrequency", "remainingTicks"(optional), "consumptionCount", "onlyExternal". See Prospector.lua for example.

text: Sets the maximum amount of parallel crafts, 65535 if not set.
params: The number of parallel crafts allowed to be done at once.
